briefs.title: Chief Election Officer to Be Appointed for 2026 Local Elections
briefs.summary: A Chief Election Officer will be appointed for the 2026 local elections. This person will be in charge of running the election.

briefs.title: North Road BIA Renewal Approved Despite Petitions
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) despite receiving petitions against it. A BIA helps local businesses by promoting the area and managing improvements. The renewal means the BIA will continue to operate and collect levies (fees) from businesses within its boundaries.
briefs.action_required: Review BIA activities to see how they benefit your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority's Corporate Maintenance Matters Need Council Approval
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby needs to get Council approval for annual corporate maintenance matters related to the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Presents 2025 Financials, 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) presented its financial results for 2025 and its budget for 2026. This shows how the BHA is managing money and planning for the future.

briefs.title: E-Bike, Scooter Share Bylaw Amendments Proposed
briefs.summary: Proposed changes to the rules for e-bike and electric kick scooter share programs are being considered. These changes would affect how these programs operate.

briefs.title: BC Housing Target Progress Report Submitted to Ministry
briefs.summary: The BC government has received a progress report on its housing target order. The report outlines the progress made towards meeting the province's housing goals. The government will use this information to track progress and make adjustments as needed.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC: Authorize Participation in Year 7
briefs.summary: The Building Benchmark BC program helps building owners compare their energy and water use to similar buildings. This helps identify ways to save money and reduce environmental impact. The council is seeking authorization to participate in Year 7 of the program.
briefs.action_required: Review the Building Benchmark BC program details and decide whether to participate.

briefs.title: FortisBC Gas Line Work Approved on Pandora St, Gaglardi Way
briefs.summary: FortisBC received approval for a statutory right-of-way. This allows them to install gas lines on Pandora Street and Gaglardi Way. A statutory right-of-way gives FortisBC the legal right to use specific land for their infrastructure.

briefs.title: Council Approves Contract for Modular Housing Four-Plex Development
briefs.summary: The council has approved a contract for a modular four-plex. This means a company will build a four-unit building using pre-made sections. The goal is to provide more housing options.

briefs.title: Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: A contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement has been approved. This means the project can move forward to fix or replace the drainage structure.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert, Paving Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract for replacing a culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream under a road) and paving work on the Central Valley Greenway. This means the project can now move forward.

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Services Contract Change Requires Approval
briefs.summary: A change to your business's cellular voice and data services contract needs approval. This ensures the services and costs align with your business needs. Council approval is needed for this contract change.
briefs.action_required: Obtain Council approval for the cellular voice and data services contract change.

briefs.title: HVAC Maintenance Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a change to the contract for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance at city buildings. This means the terms of the agreement for these services have been updated.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated HVAC maintenance contract.

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription Renewal
briefs.summary: Council approval is needed to renew the Cisco WebEx licenses. This ensures continued access to the WebEx Contact Center platform.
briefs.action_required: Obtain Council approval for the contract renewal.

briefs.title: Fire Protection System Contract Renewal: Council Approval Needed
briefs.summary: A contract renewal for fire protection systems installation and maintenance needs council approval. This ensures your business continues to meet safety standards and legal requirements for fire safety.
briefs.action_required: Obtain council approval to renew your fire protection systems contract.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque Wording Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the wording and content for a plaque that will be placed at the Burnaby Lake Overpass. This plaque will commemorate the official opening of the overpass.

briefs.title: City to Review Financial Practices with External Expert
briefs.summary: The city will hire an outside expert to review its financial practices. This is to protect the city's money, keep its finances healthy, and avoid raising taxes.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about the review's progress.

briefs.title: Beresford Street Rezoning Application Approved for First Reading
briefs.summary: A rezoning application for 5035 Beresford Street has been approved to move forward. The application proposes a 20-storey apartment building. This is the first step in the rezoning process, which needs multiple approvals before construction can begin.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about public hearings and comment periods for this rezoning application.

briefs.title: Burnaby Task Force Update: Economic Growth and Business Resilience
briefs.summary: The Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience gave an update to the Burnaby Council. The task force is working to help businesses grow and be ready for future challenges.

briefs.title: Development Agreement Change: Pier No Longer Being Built
briefs.summary: A developer has given up the security (financial guarantee) they provided to the city. They will not build the planned amenity pier. The city council will decide what to do with the land instead. This means the pier won't be built as originally planned.
briefs.action_required: Monitor city council decisions about the future of the pier site.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Businesses Now Part of Business Improvement Area
briefs.summary: Burnaby created the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA). This means businesses in that area will work together to improve the local business environment. The BIA will fund projects to attract customers and make the area more appealing.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about BIA projects and activities.
